sglang.title: Make sure we always disable symm memory without dp padding
sglang.author: nvcastet
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
It is not enough for NCCL that the symmetric tensors are allocated with the same size. They also need to stay aligned across the GPUs (aka the symmetric memory pool needs to return consistent buffers across the GPUs). To avoid inserting a misalignment in the pool, this PR disables complet

sglang.title: [FIX] Always support TP > 4 for FP4 Gemm
sglang.author: danielafrimi
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
This PR enables FP4 (NVFP4) quantization to work with TP >= 4
Background
Previously, FP4 quantized models would fail to initialize with TP=4/8 due to kernel alignment requirements. The FlashInfer-CUTLASS FP4 GEMM kernels require the N/K-dimension to be divisible by 32.
Changes
This PR makes

vllm.title: [rocm][ray] Fix: Unify Ray device visibility handling across CUDA and ROCm
vllm.author: kouroshHakha
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Problem
The Ray integration in vLLM treats CUDA and ROCm platforms differently:
On CUDA: vLLM employs a workaround where Ray sets CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES to a restricted view, vLLM deletes it, then sets it to the global view. This works because CUDA/PyTorch reads the environment variable lazily.
On ROC

Also fixes ModelRunner V2 spec decode with non-async scheduling.
Unit test was written by claude code.

Add support for ColBERT late interaction models in vLLM. ColBERT is a retrieval model that uses per-token embeddings with MaxSim scoring, offering a balance between the accuracy of cross-encoders and the efficiency of bi-encoders.
Resolves #13827

vllm.title: [Perf] Optimize the performance of structured output + reasoning
vllm.author: chaunceyjiang
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose

Optimize the performance of structured output + reasoning

Move reasoner.is_reasoning_end(request.prompt_token_ids or []) from the core engine to the frontend.

fix #33215
The root cause of #33215 is the use of the parameter
"chat_template_kwargs": {"thinking": true, "enable_thinking": true

vllm.title: [KV Connector][Metrics] Do not count local prefix cache hits in connector queries
vllm.author: markmc
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Somewhat related to #28585
The vllm:external_prefix_cache_queries metric was double-counting queries by recording the total prompt tokens instead of only the tokens actually queried from the KV connector.
Example scenario:

Request with 1000 prompt tokens
Local cache finds 600 tokens
External cache
